name: web-performance-seo
description: Fix PageSpeed Insights/Lighthouse accessibility "!" errors caused by contrast audit failures (CSS filters, OKLCH/OKLAB, low opacity, gradient text, image backgrounds). Use for accessibility-driven SEO/performance debugging and remediation.
capabilities: Identify CSS patterns that cause Lighthouse/PageSpeed accessibility failures
Scan codebases for backdrop-blur, mix-blend-mode, and OKLCH/OKLAB color usage
Convert problematic CSS color spaces to accessible alternatives
Fix gradient text with proper color fallbacks
Add proper overlays for text-over-image scenarios
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: Static analysis detected external_commands patterns and weak cryptographic algorithms. Evaluation reveals these are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' are CLI command examples in markdown documentation (ripgrep commands for code scanning), not executable code. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are OKLCH/OKLAB CSS color space specifications misidentified as crypto algorithms. This skill provides documentation and code patterns for fixing accessibility contrast issues - no security risks present.
valueStatement: This skill fixes PageSpeed Insights and Lighthouse accessibility errors caused by color contrast failures. It scans for CSS patterns that trigger accessibility audit failures and provides proven fix patterns to improve your SEO performance.
limitations: Does not run Lighthouse or PageSpeed tests - only provides guidance
Cannot automatically modify files - provides patterns for manual application
Does not test the fixes locally - recommends using Lighthouse/axe for verification
Does not cover all accessibility issues - focuses specifically on color contrast

useCases.title: Fix Failed Accessibility Audits
useCases.description: When Lighthouse shows '!' instead of a score in the accessibility section, use this skill to identify and fix the CSS patterns causing the failure.

useCases.title: Improve SEO Through Accessibility
useCases.description: Accessibility is a ranking factor. Use this skill to fix contrast issues that negatively impact search engine rankings.

useCases.title: Accessibility Code Review
useCases.description: Review CSS and components for accessibility issues before deployment to catch contrast problems early.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Contrast Fix Request
promptTemplates.prompt: My Lighthouse accessibility audit shows '!' instead of a score. The color-contrast audit is failing. Help me identify and fix the CSS patterns causing this issue.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has a failing accessibility audit in Lighthouse

promptTemplates.title: OKLCH Color Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: I am using OKLCH colors in my CSS and they are causing accessibility issues in Lighthouse. Show me how to convert OKLCH to accessible HSL or RGB alternatives.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has OKLCH/OKLAB colors causing contrast failures

promptTemplates.title: Gradient Text Accessibility
promptTemplates.prompt: My website uses gradient text but Lighthouse flags color-contrast issues. Provide a pattern for gradient text with proper color fallbacks that pass accessibility audits.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has gradient text causing contrast failures

promptTemplates.title: Text Overlay on Images
promptTemplates.prompt: I have text over images and the contrast is failing accessibility audits. Show me the correct pattern for adding overlays to ensure sufficient contrast ratios.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has text over images with poor contrast

bestPractices: Always test locally with Lighthouse or axe before deploying
Raise background opacity to 0.4 or higher (prefer 0.6+)
Use forced-colors media query for Windows high contrast mode support
Verify contrast ratios meet 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text
antiPatterns: Using CSS filters or backdrop-blur without checking accessibility impact
Using OKLCH/OKLAB colors without testing in Lighthouse
Setting text color to transparent over gradient backgrounds
Placing text directly over images without proper overlay

faq.question: What does the '!' mean in Lighthouse accessibility?
faq.answer: The '!' indicates the accessibility audit failed completely and could not compute a score. This typically happens when Lighthouse cannot analyze certain CSS patterns like filters or color spaces.

faq.question: Can this skill run Lighthouse tests for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and code patterns. You need to run Lighthouse or PageSpeed Insights yourself to verify the fixes.

faq.question: Why are OKLCH colors causing issues?
faq.answer: Lighthouse uses getImageData to analyze colors, which has limited support for newer CSS color spaces like OKLCH and OKLAB. Converting to HSL or RGB ensures accurate analysis.

faq.question: What opacity level is safe for accessibility?
faq.answer: Use background opacity of 0.4 or higher. Prefer 0.6 or higher for better contrast. Tailwind classes like /10 and /20 are too low; use /40, /60, or higher.

faq.question: Does fixing accessibility actually help SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. Accessibility is a Google ranking factor. Sites with better accessibility scores may perform better in search results.

faq.question: How do I test fixes locally before deploying?
faq.answer: Use Lighthouse in Chrome DevTools or the axe browser extension. Run tests locally to verify fixes before deploying to production.
